rationale: The previous generation (g3_c0) failed catastrophically: the agent survived the full 1000 steps without landing because the per-step survival penalty (-0.1) was far too weak relative to shaping (~1.44 per step). The generated-private gap exploded to 1484.8 (52x private return). To fix this, we dramatically increase the survival penalty to -1.5 per step (making net per-step reward negative), reduce shaping to 1.0x, and increase terminal bonus to 1000. This targets: shaping ~50-100, velocity -20, angle -10, fuel -20, survival -1500, terminal 1000. The net per-step reward will be negative (~-1.5) when not landing, forcing the agent to seek the terminal bonus. This is a single major change (penalty magnitude) from g2_c0, which had private return 267.8.

components.description: Reduced shaping multiplier to 1.0 to prevent accumulation over long episodes.
Moderate penalty on horizontal and vertical velocity to encourage controlled descent.
Penalize large angle and angular velocity.
Small penalty per engine activation to encourage fuel efficiency.

components.expression: 1000.0 * float(done and obs[6] > 0.5 and obs[7] > 0.5 and abs(obs[0]) < 0.15 and abs(obs[1]) < 0.15 and abs(obs[2]) < 0.3 and abs(obs[3]) < 0.3 and abs(obs[4]) < 0.2)
components.description: Large terminal bonus (1000) for successful landing with moderate tolerances.
